name: Hampton Court Palace
title: The Tudors
note: Pictures for the big-picture opening: Henry the Eighth, his daughter Elizabeth, and the great Tudor palace.

items.caption: Henry the Eighth, the giant of the Tudor age. To marry as he wished, he broke England away from the Pope and made himself head of a new Church of England.
items.kidcaption: Henry the Eighth, the famous king with six wives. He was so determined to get his way that he started a whole new church!
items.credit: Workshop of Hans Holbein the Younger

items.caption: Henry's daughter Elizabeth the First, whose long reign became a golden age of Shakespeare and sea power. The Tudor line ended with her in 1603.
items.kidcaption: Elizabeth the First, Henry's daughter. Her time as queen was so brilliant, with explorers and Shakespeare's plays, that it's called a golden age.

items.caption: Hampton Court's Tudor gatehouse, the great red-brick palace where Henry feasted and schemed. The finest Tudor palace still standing.
items.kidcaption: The red-brick Tudor front of Hampton Court, Henry the Eighth's favourite party palace. Look for his and his wives' initials carved around it.
